metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a second deterministic proof to AC5 (or extend the existing one) that injects a broken relative link into a non-README docs/*.md file (e.g. a scratch file created and removed inline) and confirms the gate exits non-zero, proving 'ALL docs/*.md' are scanned.
findings.finding: AC5 only proves the broken-link gate fires when the bad link is in docs/README.md; it does not verify that a broken relative link in any *other* docs/*.md file (e.g. docs/architecture.md) is also caught, leaving a gap between the stated 'scan ALL docs/*.md' behavior and the deterministic proof.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC5 with an additional one-liner: `touch docs/_probe_src_$$.md && printf '[x](./_nope_$$.md)\n' >> docs/_probe_src_$$.md; python scripts/check_docs_index.py --check >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo MISS || echo CAUGHT; rm -f docs/_probe_src_$$.md` — this proves the scanner covers files other than README.md.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a deterministic proof command (analogous to the existing two) that verifies a file matching the allowlist (e.g., `docs/foo.local.md`) does NOT cause `--check` to exit non-zero, confirming false-positive suppression.
findings.finding: The allowlist AC ('The allowlist covers *.local.md and designated historical/archive docs') has no deterministic proof that allowlisted files are correctly excluded — only detection (false-negative) paths are proven; the false-positive suppression path is unverified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'The allowlist covers *.local.md and designated historical/archive docs'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC proof: `touch docs/foo.local.md; python scripts/check_docs_index.py --check >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo 'CORRECTLY_IGNORED' || echo 'FALSE_POSITIVE'; rm -f docs/foo.local.md` — expected output: `CORRECTLY_IGNORED`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add concrete proving commands for AC2 (e.g. `grep -E 'local\.md' scripts/check_docs_index.py`) and AC3 (e.g. `grep -F 'check_docs_index' .github/workflows/test.yml`) so all ACs are machine-verifiable.
findings.finding: AC2 ('The allowlist covers *.local.md and designated historical/archive docs') and AC3 ('The check is wired into CI and passes on the now-completed index') are completion-relevant claims with no concrete proving commands.
findings.suggested_fix:
AC2: append `# PROOF: grep -E 'ALLOWLIST|local\.md' scripts/check_docs_index.py | head` 
AC3: append `# PROOF: grep -F 'check_docs_index.py' .github/workflows/test.yml`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the relative-link resolver uses path-resolution semantics (resolve each relative href against the containing file's directory within docs/), not a literal `docs/` href prefix filter — update the script spec and AC5 wording to say 'relative links that resolve to a nonexistent path' rather than 'links to docs/… targets'.
findings.finding: The relative-link check description uses 'docs/… targets' but relative links inside docs/*.md resolve against the file's own location (e.g. `config.md`, `./config.md`) — they don't contain a `docs/` prefix. An implementer who filters on a literal `docs/` href prefix will miss almost all intra-docs relative links, making the broken-link guard effectively dead for the primary use case.
findings.location: What section / AC5: 'scan ALL docs/*.md for relative markdown links to docs/… targets'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'relative markdown links to docs/… targets' with 'relative markdown links that, when resolved relative to the containing file's directory, point to a path that does not exist on disk'. The check should: parse each `[text](href)` where href is relative (no scheme, no leading `/`), resolve `os.path.join(os.path.dirname(source_file), href)`, and fail if the resolved path does not exist.

findings.finding: The dead-link check mechanism is ambiguously specified as scanning for 'relative markdown links to docs/… targets' — a phrase that could be implemented as literal `docs/`-prefix href matching, which would miss virtually all actual intra-docs relative links (which look like `[foo](bar.md)` or `[foo](../adr/bar.md)`, not `[foo](docs/bar.md)`). The correct mechanism must resolve each href relative to the linking file's filesystem location and check whether the resolved path exists — a meaningfully different algorithm.
findings.location: What / scripts/check_docs_index.py bullet (b): 'scan ALL docs/*.md for relative markdown links to docs/… targets'
findings.suggested_fix: Restate part (b) as: 'for each docs/*.md file, extract all relative markdown link hrefs (excluding http/https/mailto and anchor-only `#…` links), resolve each href against the linking file's directory using `Path.resolve()`, and exit non-zero if the resolved path does not exist on disk.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the templated CI pattern: either update the plan to say the new script uses the `--check` flag style (distinct from the `git diff` style used in the actual test.yml env-var gate), or update AC4/AC5 to match the `git diff`-based invocation — they must be consistent.
findings.finding: The plan says the established pattern from `gen_env_registry.py` is 'a small script invoked from `.github/workflows/test.yml` that fails on divergence', implying the CI step calls the script with `--check`. But the actual `test.yml` step (lines 255-262) invokes `python scripts/gen_env_registry.py` (regenerate in-place) and then uses `git diff --exit-code` to detect divergence — it never calls `--check`. The plan's ACs for the new script use `--check`. An implementer templating directly from the CI step will produce a `git diff`-based gate rather than a `--check`-based gate.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section: 'Template the established pattern'; AC3 and AC4/AC5 (use `--check`)
findings.suggested_fix: Explicitly note that the new script adopts the `--check` exit-code style (present in gen_env_registry.py's argparse but not used by test.yml) rather than the regenerate+`git diff` style. Update the CI wiring description accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the script's CLI contract in the 'What' section — specifically document the `--check` flag (or equivalent) its behavior and that the proving commands in AC4/AC5 invoke it correctly.
findings.finding: The `--check` flag is used in both deterministic proving commands (AC4 and AC5) but is never defined in the script specification in the 'What' section. An executor who implements `check_docs_index.py` without that flag will produce proving commands that either silently pass (wrong-helper-script-argv taxonomy item 3) or error out, making the ACs unverifiable as written.
findings.location: AC4 and AC5 proving commands; 'What' section script spec for `scripts/check_docs_index.py`
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the `scripts/check_docs_index.py` spec bullet: 'The script accepts a `--check` flag; when passed, it runs both checks and exits non-zero on any failure (default mode if no flag is given can also run checks — either way, document which invocation form the CI step and proving commands use).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sentence to the 'What' section specifying the script's invocation interface — at minimum, whether `--check` is the only/default mode or whether multiple modes exist — so the body and AC proof commands reference the same entity.
findings.finding: The script's CLI interface (specifically the `--check` flag) is used in both deterministic proof ACs but is never described in the plan body's script specification, leaving the implementer without a specified invocation contract.
findings.location: AC4 and AC5 (deterministic proof commands) vs. 'What' section script description
findings.suggested_fix: In the script description, add: 'The script accepts a `--check` flag (or runs in check mode by default); invoked as `python scripts/check_docs_index.py --check`, it exits non-zero on any violation and prints a diagnostic. No fix/write mode is required.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and correct the `*.local.md` git-ignored claim: `.gitignore` only ignores `CLAUDE.local.md` (literal), not `docs/*.local.md` as a glob — either add `docs/*.local.md` to `.gitignore` or restate the allowlist as a filename-pattern match (not a git-ignore check) to avoid the script being implemented incorrectly.
findings.finding: The plan claims `*.local.md` files are 'git-ignored' to justify their place on the allowlist, but `.gitignore` only lists the literal filename `CLAUDE.local.md` — not the glob `docs/*.local.md` or `*.local.md`. A file named `docs/notes.local.md` would NOT be ignored by git, making the allowlist justification rest on a false premise.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section: allowlist rationale; AC2: 'The allowlist covers `*.local.md` and designated historical/archive docs'
findings.suggested_fix: Either add `docs/*.local.md` to `.gitignore` so the claim becomes accurate, OR restate the plan to say the allowlist matches files by name suffix (`*.local.md`) rather than by git-ignored status, and implement it as a `fnmatch`/`endswith` check in the script.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` to the Files list and add the `python scripts/check_docs_index.py --check` step there, mirroring the `test.yml` wiring (following the established pattern of `gen_env_registry.py` and `check_server_manifest.py`).
findings.finding: `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` is an unenumerated consumer that will need updating. Every gate script wired into `test.yml` is also mirrored verbatim in `gerrit-verify.yaml` (confirmed for `gen_env_registry.py` and `check_server_manifest.py`). The plan's Files list names only `scripts/check_docs_index.py` (new) and `.github/workflows/test.yml`, silently omitting `gerrit-verify.yaml`. A new docs-index gate wired only into `test.yml` but not `gerrit-verify.yaml` would let a Gerrit patchset that breaks the docs index pass the Verified vote.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Files line: `scripts/check_docs_index.py` (new), `.github/workflows/test.yml`, `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`. In `gerrit-verify.yaml`, add a step `python scripts/check_docs_index.py --check` alongside the other drift-gate steps in the `build-and-test` job, following the same shell pattern used for `check_server_manifest.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the AC5 proof command quoting: use double quotes or `$'...'` so `$$` expands to the PID as intended, e.g. `printf '[x](./_nope_%s.md)\n' "$$" >> docs/README.md`, or simply use a fixed unique name like `_nope_broken_link_probe_.md` to avoid shell quoting complexity.
findings.finding:
AC5's proof command uses single-quoted printf: `printf '[x](./_nope_$$.md)
'`. Single quotes in bash suppress all expansions, so `$$` is the two-character literal string `$$` (not the PID) and `
` is two characters `
`, not a newline — in many shells `printf` will still interpret `
` as a newline escape, but the behavior is implementation-dependent and the intent (uniqueness via PID) is not achieved.
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace `printf '[x](./_nope_$$.md)
'` with `printf '[x](./_nope_broken_link_probe_.md)
'` (no PID needed since the cleanup is `git checkout -- docs/README.md`) or with `printf "[x](./_nope_$$.md)
"` (double quotes to allow `$$` expansion).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief rationale (in the plan's 'What' section or the script's docstring) for why a bespoke stdlib-only parser is preferred over an established tool such as `lychee` (local-path mode) or `markdown-link-check` — e.g. 'no Node/Rust CI dependency, scope is limited to `docs/` relative paths only, and the index-completeness logic is repo-specific' — so the choice is recorded as deliberate rather than inadvertent reinvention.
findings.finding: The plan commits to a bespoke Python relative-link parser (script part b: 'scan ALL docs/*.md for relative markdown links to docs/… targets and exit non-zero on any that do not resolve') without acknowledging established tools (lychee, markdown-link-check/mlc) that already do exactly this, and without recording why bespoke was preferred over them.
findings.location: What section — script description part (b): 'scan ALL docs/*.md for relative markdown links to docs/… targets and exit non-zero on any that do not resolve to an existing file (general broken-relative-link guard within docs/)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the 'What' section, e.g.: 'A bespoke stdlib-only implementation is used (no Node/Rust CI dependency) because the scope is narrow (relative `docs/`-internal links only) and the index-completeness allowlist logic is repo-specific; lychee/markdown-link-check were considered but add an external toolchain for minimal gain at this scope.'

findings.finding: AC4 relies on 'manual verification' rather than a deterministic proving command, making it a hedge that cannot be autonomously confirmed by an agent or CI.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4: 'Adding a new unindexed doc is demonstrably caught (note manual verification).'
findings.suggested_fix: AC4: 'Verified by: `touch docs/orphan_test.md && python scripts/check_docs_index.py` exits non-zero (exit code 1); remove the file afterward. This confirms the failure path.'

findings.finding: The plan asserts that ticket `9d81` relocates files under `docs/archive/`, motivating the dead-link check for 'inbound links broken by 9d81's docs/archive/ relocations'. However, ticket `9d81` explicitly states 'We deliberately choose NOT to relocate any file' and 'No file moves → no inbound link can break.' No `docs/archive/` relocations are performed by `9d81`, so the factual premise for the relative-link scan (catching broken inbound links from `9d81`'s moves) is incorrect.
findings.location: Plan body: 'inbound links from any docs/*.md to files that 9d81 relocates under docs/archive/' and AC line 5
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '9d81 relocates under docs/archive/' with a general rationale such as: 'relative markdown links within docs/ resolve — catching any broken links introduced by file renames or future relocations.'

findings.finding: `gerrit-verify.yaml` is an unenumerated edit target. The existing parity test `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py::test_no_drift_script_gate_is_verified_only_in_branch_ci` scans `test.yml` for `scripts/*.py` references and asserts every one is also present in `gerrit-verify.yaml`. Adding `scripts/check_docs_index.py` to `test.yml` without also adding it to `gerrit-verify.yaml` will fail this test, and more importantly will leave the gate as post-merge-only (the same class of defect the parity test was written to catch).
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Files: `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` (add docs-index gate step mirroring its test.yml counterpart).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Externalize the allowlist (e.g., to a `.docs-index-allowlist` file or a clearly-delimited top-of-file constant with a comment) so non-script authors can update it without touching the script logic.
findings.finding: The allowlist (covering `*.local.md` and designated historical/archive docs) is expected to grow over time as new archive docs are designated, but the plan does not specify whether it is externalized to a config file or hardcoded inside the script body. A hardcoded allowlist requires editing the script source for every future archive addition, violating the changeability sub-check of T5e.
findings.location: Plan body: 'The allowlist covers `*.local.md` and designated historical/archive docs' / `scripts/check_docs_index.py` (new)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a top-of-file `ALLOWLIST` constant (e.g., `ALLOWLIST = ['*.local.md', 'ARCHIVE_*.md']`) with a comment directing editors there, OR read the allowlist from a `.docs-index-allowlist` text file in the repo root, making the update path obvious to doc authors.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the complete precondition for AC3: either (a) name which currently-unindexed living docs (e.g. `attest-substrate.md`, `review-policy.md`, `dco-rollout-evidence.md`, `session-id-shims.md`) must be added to docs/README.md as part of this work, or (b) explicitly list them on the allowlist with a stated rationale beyond 'historical/archive', so the implementer knows exactly what 'the now-completed index' means.
findings.finding: The plan states the gate 'passes on the (now-completed) index' (AC3) and the allowlist covers 'historical/archive docs', but at least four living top-level docs/*.md files are currently absent from docs/README.md and are not archive/historical material: `attest-substrate.md`, `review-policy.md`, `dco-rollout-evidence.md`, `session-id-shims.md`. The plan does not tell the implementer whether these must be added to the index as part of this work or added to the allowlist (with what rationale), leaving an unresolved precondition that will cause the gate to fail CI immediately if not addressed.
findings.location: AC3: 'The check is wired into CI and passes on the (now-completed) index.' / Allowlist section: 'The allowlist covers *.local.md and designated historical/archive docs.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
